metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert the promotion-path documentation requirement into a repo artifact (e.g. a `docs/plan-review-g7-promotion.md` stub or a DECISIONS entry) and add a proving command (`ls docs/plan-review-g7-promotion.md`) so the AC is in-session verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4's 'the promotion decision path is documented on this ticket' is a post-implementation editorial action with no in-session proving command or artifact spec — it cannot be verified at claim time and is not a durable code/repo end-state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'a `docs/decisions/g7-promotion-criteria.md` file documenting the field-data thresholds and window that will trigger the blocking promotion review.' Then the AC becomes: 'The promotion criteria file exists at docs/decisions/g7-promotion-criteria.md and states the observation window and fire-rate/adjudication threshold for promotion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `orchestrator.py` (`_assemble_context_uncached` or a new parent-fetch helper) and/or `det_floor.py` (`PlanContext`) as edit targets for the parent-plan context injection, consistent with how ISF and G5 inject supporting context.
findings.finding: The parent-plan context assembly requires fetching the parent ticket's description at review time, but neither `orchestrator.py`'s `_assemble_context_uncached` nor `det_floor.py`'s `PlanContext` are named as edit targets. The AC states 'The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context (bounded to parent + grandparent), with a test asserting the assembly,' implying a real assembly-side code change, yet the Scope section lists only `criteria_routing.json`, `registry.py`, the rubric prompt, and the move registry.
findings.location: Scope section / AC5 / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py (_assemble_context_uncached) and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (PlanContext)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the reference to the non-existent 'ancestor carve-out / 8a9bb4308' with a concrete spec: which function fetches parent (and optionally grandparent) plan text, how it is passed to the G7 agent's context (system prompt extension, extra injected blob, or a new PlanContext field), and the token-budget rule that bounds its size.
findings.finding: The plan's context-assembly spec says parent plan text is injected 'the same way the ancestor carve-out reads ancestors — bounded, no whole-subtree dumps' and references commit '8a9bb4308', but no such ancestor carve-out mechanism exists in the codebase. `_ticket_graph_blob` injects only the parent's ticket ID (a string), not its plan text. No existing code path fetches and injects a parent's description/title/ACs into an individual criterion's agentic context window.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph / AC line 5 ('parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'the same way the ancestor carve-out reads ancestors' with a concrete description of the new injection mechanism (e.g., 'pre-fetch parent ticket via `show_ticket(parent_id)` in pass1.py before the criterion agent call, inject title+description as a bounded context block — analogous to how `_ticket_graph_blob` assembles the ISF graph blob').
findings.finding: The plan asserts context assembly should inject parent plan text 'the same way the ancestor carve-out reads ancestors,' implying a pre-existing named context-injection mechanism for ancestors. No such mechanism exists in pass1.py or the orchestrator — the G1G2 'ancestor carve-out' is purely an agentic LLM instruction (rubric text telling the agent to treat depends_on targets as existing), not a pre-assembled context-injection pipeline. The plan's 'same way' invocation describes a non-existent implementation pattern.
findings.location: CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section: 'inject the parent's title/description (and the parent's own parent one level up, if present) into the criterion's context the same way the ancestor carve-out reads ancestors — bounded, no whole-subtree dumps'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to a concrete routing design for the `has_parent_id` gate: either (a) add a `requires_parent: true` key to the `applies_at` schema and extend `registry.applies()` to accept `has_parent_id`, threading it through `route_criteria()` and `workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria`; or (b) add an explicit imperative branch in `route_criteria()` for G7 (precedent: the ISF special-case at orchestrator.py:351-352). Document the chosen approach in the Scope section so the routing test in AC1 can be written against a known contract.
findings.finding: The `applies_at` schema and `registry.applies()` have no `has_parent_id` axis, so the plan's required routing gate ('ticket has parent_id AND no children') cannot be expressed declaratively today. The plan hedges this as 'if not expressible declaratively → registry.py' but does not commit to a design: (a) add a new `requires_parent` key to the `applies_at` schema and extend `applies()`, (b) add an imperative branch in `route_criteria()` (precedent: ISF special-case), or (c) rely on the AGENT rubric to self-gate via PASS not-applicable for parentless leaves. Options (a) and (b) require extending the routing machinery; option (c) would silently run the criterion on ALL leaves (incurring AGENT-tier cost) and rely on the model to self-suppress — a different posture than the plan's AC ('a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets'). The AC explicitly demands a routing-level skip, which rules out (c), making the required extension un-specified.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py (applies_at leaf gate if not expressible declaratively)'; AC1: 'a routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `requires_parent: true` to the G7 `applies_at` block in criteria_routing.json, extend `registry.applies()` to accept `has_parent_id: bool = False` and check `if ap.get('requires_parent') and not has_parent_id: return False`, then thread `has_parent_id=bool(ctx.state.get('parent_id'))` through the three call sites (orchestrator.route_criteria, workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria, production_batch_runner). This is purely additive — existing criteria have no `requires_parent` key so the default (False → no constraint) is backward-compatible.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC4 that runs G7 against a fixture leaf ticket and asserts the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar contains the fire-rate (or equivalent result) field, e.g. `jq -e '.g7_result != null' <sidecar-path>`.
findings.finding: AC4's 'fire-rate observable in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars' has no concrete proving command — there is no step or fixture that runs G7 on a test ticket and inspects the sidecar to confirm the fire-rate field is present and populated, so the executor cannot verify observability in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the `parent_id` gate is enforced in the routing layer: either add a `has_parent: bool` parameter to `registry.applies` (and update `route_criteria` + its callers) or confirm the AGENT self-gates via a tool call and the criterion runs (at advisory cost) on all leaves.
findings.finding: The plan says G7 fires only on '(ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)' but does not specify how the parent_id condition is enforced in the routing layer. The `applies` function currently accepts (has_children, ticket_type, plan) — there is no `has_parent` parameter, and `route_criteria` does not pass `ctx.state.get("parent_id")` to it. The plan acknowledges this with 'registry.py (applies_at leaf gate if not expressible declaratively)' but leaves the mechanism unresolved: either (a) `applies` needs a new `has_parent: bool` parameter and `route_criteria` must be updated to pass it, or (b) the applies_at gate is dropped and the AGENT self-gates by checking parent_id in its tool loop (incurring cost on parentless leaves). These paths have different blast radii and test requirements.
findings.location: What section — ROUTING paragraph; Scope — registry.py applies_at leaf gate; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add `has_parent: bool = False` to `registry.applies` and pass `has_parent=bool(ctx.state.get('parent_id'))` in `route_criteria`. This is the minimal, pattern-consistent change (mirrors how `has_children` gates container-only criteria) and keeps the routing test straightforward.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether the current `applies_at` schema in `applies()` can express the `has_parent_id` gate declaratively, and if not, specify the exact extension needed (e.g. a new `require_fields: [parent_id]` condition or a custom `applies_at` key) so the implementer has a clear contract before coding.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'applies_at gates on (ticket has a parent_id) AND (ticket has no children)' but the current applies() function in registry.py only supports has_children (via scope), suppress_types, and suppress_when — it has no has_parent_id condition. Whether this gate can be expressed declaratively in the routing JSON or requires new applies() logic is left as '(if not expressible declaratively)' without verifying the current schema's actual capability.
findings.location: What section / ROUTING bullet; also Scope: 'registry.py (applies_at leaf gate if not expressible declaratively)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `require_parent: true` key to the `applies_at` block in the routing entry and extend `applies()` to check `ctx.state.get('parent_id')` (or equivalent) when that key is present. Document the new key in the routing schema comment. This is a one-line addition to `applies()` but needs to be spec'd explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact extension to `registry.applies()` (new `has_parent: bool` parameter), the corresponding `applies_at` key or `suppress_when` condition name, and every call site in orchestrator/production_batch_runner that must pass the new argument.
findings.finding: The G7 `applies_at` gate requires `ticket has a parent_id AND no children`, but the existing `registry.applies()` function has no `has_parent` parameter and the `applies_at` schema supports only `scope`, `suppress_types`, and `suppress_when`. The plan acknowledges the gap parenthetically ('applies_at leaf gate if not expressible declaratively') but never specifies the fallback mechanism: where in the call-chain the `has_parent` check lives, what signature extension is needed, or how the gate signals non-applicability when parent_id is absent.
findings.location: Scope section / registry.py applies_at gate / AC line 1 ('routing test proves it is skipped for parentless tickets')
findings.suggested_fix: Add `has_parent: bool = False` to `registry.applies()`, define a new `suppress_when` condition name (e.g. `no_parent`) recognized there, add `"suppress_when": ["no_parent"]` to G7's `applies_at`, and enumerate all call sites (orchestrator.py, production_batch_runner.py) that must pass `has_parent=bool(ctx.state.get('parent_id'))` to `applies()`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the error-handling posture for parent fetch failure: if the parent record is missing or the store is unavailable, the criterion must degrade gracefully (e.g., emit a structured SKIP/WARN rather than pass silently or raise), and log the failure so operators can observe it.
findings.finding: The plan introduces an LLM call (exec: AGENT) and a ticket-store read for the parent record, but specifies no error-handling posture for the case where the parent fetch fails (store unavailable, orphaned parent_id pointing to a deleted/missing ticket). It is unspecified whether the criterion should skip gracefully (PASS-through), surface an error finding, or block the review — leaving a silent failure path.
findings.location: What section — 'CONTEXT ASSEMBLY: inject the parent's title/description… bounded, no whole-subtree dumps'; Acceptance Criteria — 'The parent's plan text is injected into the criterion context'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single sentence to the CONTEXT ASSEMBLY section: 'If the parent record cannot be fetched (missing, store error), the criterion emits result=SKIP with reason='parent_unavailable' and logs a warning; it does not silently pass or hard-error.' Mirror whatever the ancestor carve-out does today and cite it explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behavior when the G7 agent cannot fetch the parent ticket: PASS not-applicable (with a coverage note), INDETERMINATE (recorded in coverage), or a DET-style failure finding — and add it to the rubric prompt and the Scope section.
findings.finding: G7 is `exec: AGENT` and must fetch the parent ticket from the store at review time, but no fallback is specified for parent-fetch failure (parent deleted, store unavailable, network error, or parent_id present but unresolvable). Analogous AGENT criteria (G1G2, E4) that fail on store reads either raise or return empty findings; for G7, an unresolvable parent renders the criterion vacuous, but the plan does not say whether to PASS not-applicable, INDETERMINATE, or emit a finding.
findings.location: What section — CONTEXT ASSEMBLY paragraph / ROUTING section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the parent-context field spec: state explicitly which fields are injected (e.g. title + description + What section + ACs, or the full plan text), and use that same field list consistently in both the What section and AC5.
findings.finding: The What section says inject 'parent's title/description' but AC5 says inject 'parent's plan text' — these are different fields and the inconsistency leaves the executor to decide which fields to actually inject, which is a real design decision (title+description vs full What/Scope/ACs).
findings.location: What section (CONTEXT ASSEMBLY bullet) vs AC5
findings.suggested_fix: In both the What section and AC5, replace the ambiguous 'title/description' / 'plan text' with a concrete field list, e.g.: 'inject the parent's title, description, What, Scope, and Acceptance Criteria sections (and the same fields from the grandparent if present), excluding attachments and session logs, to stay within the bounded-context intent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the G7 coaching move's id, name, template string (with `{subject}` placeholder encoding the parent-wins rule), and `applies_when` tag(s) in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan mandates 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move)' but never specifies the move's id, name, or template string. The `validate_move_registry` schema requires both `name` (non-empty string) and `template` (string containing exactly one `{subject}` placeholder). Without these, an executor cannot add a schema-valid entry — and the `applies_when` tags that gate move applicability to G7 findings also go unspecified.
findings.location: Scope section — 'the coach MOVE_REGISTRY (one parent-realign move)' / AC line 3 ('rubric and the Pass-4 coaching move state the parent-wins rule')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to MOVE_REGISTRY: `"13": {"name": "parent-realign", "template": "Realign {subject} to the parent's declared scope, or update the parent first if the parent is wrong.", "applies_when": ["G7"]}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update the verifier rubric (`plan_review_verifier.md` and `plan_review_verifier_agentic.md`) to extend `divergent_implementation` to cover parent-plan divergence (G7 findings), or specify a different existing axis that maps correctly without a rubric change — and add this to the Scope section.
findings.finding: The plan maps G7 findings to the `divergent_implementation` severity axis in Pass-2/3, but that axis is defined in `verify.py` as 'the plan diverges from the implementation or reality it claims to describe.' A G7 finding is leaf-plan-diverges-from-parent-plan — a different relationship. No verifier rubric update or sub-answer re-mapping protocol is specified, so the verifier will grade G7 findings against the wrong semantic definition, systematically mis-scoring them.
findings.location: What section — 'severity maps onto the existing plan axes (divergent_implementation is the natural home)' / Pass-2/3 pipeline

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sub-clause to AC4 requiring a test that asserts the criterion's configured posture is advisory and threshold is 0.95, matching the Scope's named 'posture pin' test.
findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly names a 'posture pin' test as a deliverable, but no Acceptance Criterion requires a test asserting the advisory posture is configured at 0.95 — the posture pin test is an uncovered deliverable.
findings.location: Scope section ('tests (routing: ...; semantics: ...; posture pin)') vs AC4 ('ships advisory (0.95) with fire-rate observable in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars; the promotion decision path is documented on this ticket')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4 to: 'The criterion ships advisory (0.95) with fire-rate observable in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars; a test asserts the registered posture value is advisory at 0.95; the promotion decision path is documented on this ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the semantic fixture tests invoke the live LLM agent (preferred) or a stub; if a stub is used, add at least one live/end-to-end acceptance criterion (e.g., a manual or CI smoke test) that exercises the real rubric prompt against the three fixture cases to confirm the criterion fires and passes correctly in production.
findings.finding: The semantic fixture tests (leaf narrowing → PASS; leaf adding out-of-scope → fires; leaf contradicting parent AC → fires) are specified without stating whether they invoke the live LLM agent or a mock/stub. If these tests use a canned/fake agent that never calls the real rubric prompt, the test suite can go green while the G7 rubric prompt is entirely broken — a proxy-validation of the new LLM-executed behavior.
findings.location: Scope section / AC line 2: 'Fixture-driven tests: a leaf narrowing its parent PASSES; a leaf adding out-of-parent-scope deliverables fires; a leaf contradicting a parent AC fires.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or note: 'Semantic fixture tests run against the live agent in CI (or a designated integration-test target); a documented rationale is required if a stub is used instead, explaining why the substitute faithfully represents the rubric prompt's behavior.' Alternatively, split: keep routing/context-assembly as unit tests (stub fine) and add one integration-level test (live agent, clearly tagged) for the three semantic fixture cases.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or remove the commit hash '8a9bb4308' with a verifiable reference (ADR number, rubric file path, or section name) that grounds the 'ancestor carve-out' the plan builds on.
findings.finding: The plan cites commit hash '8a9bb4308' as the authority for the 'depends_on-ancestor carve-out' it builds on, but this hash has zero matches anywhere in the codebase or ADR directory — it cannot be verified that it describes what the plan claims, and the actual carve-out behavior (the G1G2 agentic depends_on exemption) is only documented in the rubric prompt text, not tied to this identifier.
findings.location: Why section: 'the depends_on-ancestor carve-out (8a9bb4308) handles deliverable-existence, not scope containment'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(8a9bb4308)' with '(see plan_review_G1G2.md rubric: depends_on carve-out)' or the relevant ADR if one exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `registry.py`'s `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset (and the criteria guide regeneration) to the named edit targets in the Scope section.
findings.finding: `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset in `registry.py` is not named as an edit target, but adding G7 to `criteria_routing.json` without also adding it to `CANONICAL_LLM` will cause `validate_packaged_routing()` to flag G7 as an ORPHAN routing entry (keys − CANONICAL_LLM check at line 439), failing the CI parity gate.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py line 79 (CANONICAL_LLM frozenset)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, explicitly add: `registry.CANONICAL_LLM` (add `'G7'`); run `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry regenerate-criteria-guide` to update `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` in `registry.py` to the named edit targets, and include `'G7'` in that frozenset.
findings.finding: `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` frozenset in `registry.py` is an unenumerated consumer. G7 reads the parent ticket from the store at review time (like G1G2/E4/A1/G6), yet the plan does not name `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` as an edit target. Omitting G7 from this set means `attest.py`'s `code_grounded` attestation signal and `workflow_ops.py`'s `code_grounded` verdict flag will not recognize G7 findings as codebase-grounded, affecting re-review staleness and drift-refresh behavior.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py line 66 (CODEBASE_GROUNDED frozenset)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, add `registry.CODEBASE_GROUNDED` (add `'G7'`) alongside the other registry.py edits.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a durable doc artifact (ADR addendum or CLAUDE.md section) capturing the parent-wins conflict rule rationale and the promotion-decision criteria/evidence path, so future maintainers have a non-ephemeral reference.
findings.finding: The 'parent-wins' conflict resolution rule and the observation-window-to-promotion pathway are novel architectural/policy decisions, but the plan documents them only on the ticket itself (ephemeral). No ADR addendum or CLAUDE.md update is specified, meaning future maintainers have no durable reference for why parent-wins was chosen or what the promotion threshold criteria are.
findings.location: Plan § POSTURE and § What (CONFLICT RULE); AC line 'file the promotion decision as a comment on this ticket'
findings.suggested_fix: Either add a lightweight ADR (e.g., docs/adr/XXXX-g7-parent-containment.md) covering (1) why parent-wins rather than leaf-wins, (2) the advisory-to-blocking promotion criteria, and (3) the observation-window commitment; OR add a dedicated section to CLAUDE.md under plan-review criteria. Reference it from the ticket comment rather than putting the full decision only there.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the AGENT tier choice (e.g. 'AGENT is chosen because the grandparent lookup and bounded-read resilience require tool-loop flexibility beyond what extra_context pre-assembly can provide').
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for choosing the AGENT execution tier over the 1-TURN + extra_context injection pattern (which G5 already uses for store-derived context). The only hint is 'it must read the parent ticket from the store, like the other codebase-grounded criteria' — but the G5 decomposition injection shows that store-fetched context CAN be pre-assembled and injected as extra_context into a 1-TURN call, which is ~85× cheaper per the existing cost model. Without a stated rationale, an implementer cannot tell whether AGENT was chosen for a specific reason (e.g. bounded multi-hop grandparent lookup, resilience to large parent plans) or by analogy.
findings.location: What section — first bullet ('exec: AGENT'); Scope — 'a new reviewers/plan-review-g7 rubric prompt'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
